panel.basis: log10(Vmax mult) vs n_C R^2 0.70 (<0.9) and vs log K_PL 0.62 -- not a clean log-linear law.
multiplier is ~1.2x flat to C10 then jumps 2.0x (C11), 5.5x (C12) -- threshold-like, not smooth/predictable.

chief.basis: The carrier-enhancement factor is NOT a smooth log-linear function of chain length (R^2 0.70 on n_C, 0.62 on K_PL; both <0.9): essentially no enhancement is needed to C10, then a steep onset at C11-C12. So the long-chain active-carrier enhancement is NOT cleanly QSPR-able -- it stays a longest-chain-specific (ad-hoc) lever, not a chain-length-predictable one. Hypothesis refuted.
